query: How active is the 2026 US tornado season and what is the forecast/outlook for early July 2026 severe weather? Current ENSO state (La Nina/El Nino) for summer 2026 and any Climate Prediction Center outlook for July 2026 severe convective weather in northern Plains and Midwest.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings:

**2026 US Tornado Season — Very Active:** ~1,100–1,141 preliminary tornado reports through late June with 78 fatalities. Above the ~1,225/year average pace but below record 2024/2025. Illinois anomalously leads the nation. March 2026 was the 3rd most active March on record (204). The May 19–26 super outbreak produced 162 tornadoes in 24 hours (2nd-largest single-day count ever), including the first Minnesota EF5.

**Early/Mid-July 2026 Severe Weather — Quiet, Heat-Dominated:** The July 13 SPC Day-1 outlook had only Marginal Risk for the Southern Atlantic Seaboard and western Montana — no northern Plains/Midwest severe area. Day 4–8 showed no confident severe areas (large model spread), with only a possible future Upper Midwest–Mid-Atlantic risk for Days 6–8. CPC's Week-2 outlook is dominated by extreme heat, not severe convection.

**Summer 2026 ENSO — El Niño (strengthening), NOT La Niña:** This is the key correction — some search results surfaced an outdated La Niña Advisory tweet, but the current state is a strengthening El Niño. CPC's July 9 discussion has an El Niño Advisory in effect (Niño-3.4 +1.2°C), with 81% odds of a very strong El Niño by Oct–Dec 2026. IRI shows ~100% El Niño through summer/fall. For the northern Plains/Midwest, CPC's July outlook gives "Equal Chances" for temperature and precipitation, treating El Niño as a weak secondary factor given weak summer teleconnections.

Full report with all sources and quantitative data points has been submitted via the structured output.

sources.title: Tornadoes of 2026: Season Tracker, 78 Deaths, Illinois Leads
sources.snippet: More than 1,100 preliminary tornado reports through late June, 78 dead; May 19-26 super outbreak produced 162 tornadoes in 24 hours.

sources.title: List of United States tornadoes from January to March 2026
sources.snippet: January 21 confirmed, February 49, March 204 — 3rd most active March on record behind 2022 and 2025.

sources.title: CPC ENSO Diagnostic Discussion (issued July 9, 2026)
sources.snippet: El Niño Advisory in effect; latest weekly Niño-3.4 +1.2°C; 81% chance very strong El Niño Oct-Dec 2026; 97% persistence into early spring 2027.

sources.title: IRI ENSO Forecast — June 2026 Quick Look
sources.snippet: Niño 3.4 +0.94°C in May 2026, +1.7°C week of June 17; ~100% El Niño probability JJA/JAS/ASO/SON 2026.

sources.title: SPC Day 1 Convective Outlook (July 13, 2026)
sources.snippet: Marginal Risk of severe thunderstorms across parts of the Southern Atlantic Seaboard and western Montana.

sources.title: SPC Day 4-8 Severe Weather Outlook (valid July 16-21, 2026)
sources.snippet: No unconditional severe probabilities due to model spread; possible risk area Upper Midwest to Mid-Atlantic Days 6-8.

sources.title: CPC US Hazards Outlook (Week-2, valid July 20-26, 2026)
sources.snippet: Persistent Four Corners high pressure brings extreme heat risk to much of western, central, and southeastern CONUS.

sources.title: CPC 30-Day Outlook Discussion for July 2026
sources.snippet: Equal Chances temperature/precip for upper Midwest and Great Lakes; El Niño Advisory active but a secondary factor as summer teleconnections are weak.

sources.title: ENSO Conditions and Coral Bleaching / NWS PNS 26-05 (RONI)
sources.snippet: Effective February 1, 2026, the Relative Oceanic Niño Index (RONI) is used for official ENSO monitoring and prediction.

sources.title: NOAA SPC WCM — Severe Weather Maps, Graphics, and Data (2026 preliminary reports)
sources.snippet: SPC page hosting 2026 preliminary tornado reports to date and ENSO-phase tornado climatology.
